# apsum_ellip_ann_exact { #astroapers.apsum_ellip_ann_exact }

```python
apsum_ellip_ann_exact(
    data,
    x,
    y,
    a_in,
    b_in,
    a_out,
    b_out,
    theta_in=0.0,
    theta_out=None,
    *,
    mask=None,
    return_npix=True,
)
```

Return exact elliptical-annulus aperture sums for one or many aperture centers.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**data**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Two-dimensional image. For ``mask=None``, ``float64``, ``float32``, ``int32``, and ``int16`` arrays dispatch to dtype-specialized Rust kernels when available; other dtypes are converted to contiguous ``float64`` before summation. Returned aperture sums and effective pixel counts are always ``float64`` arrays.

<code>[**x, y**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [scalar or array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Aperture center coordinates in pixel units. Inputs are converted to contiguous ``float64`` arrays. Shapes must match after ``numpy.atleast_1d``. The return shape matches that broadcast-free input shape, so scalar inputs return one-element arrays.

<code>[**a_in, b_in, a_out, b_out**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Inner and outer ellipse semiaxes in pixels. Inner axes must be smaller than the corresponding outer axes.

<code>[**theta_in**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Inner ellipse rotation angle in radians.

<code>[**theta_out**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float or None, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Outer ellipse rotation angle in radians. If `None`, uses ``theta_in``.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with the same shape as ``data``. ``True`` pixels are excluded from both the aperture sum and effective pixel count. Values are converted to boolean. When this is omitted, the unmasked Rust summation path is used.

<code>[**return_npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `True`, return ``(apsum, npix)``. If `False`, return only ``apsum`` and use the sum-only Rust kernel for supported unmasked calls.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**apsum**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sum of the unmasked data values multiplied by the aperture weights.

<code>[**npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Effective in-frame pixel count, returned only when ``return_npix=True``. This is the sum of aperture weights after image clipping and mask exclusion.

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

For matched inner and outer angles, this uses outer-minus-inner direct aperture summation. For split-angle annuli, it uses Rust-generated bbox-tight annulus weights and the same reduction as ``ApMask.apsum``.
